title: Capillary Action in Plants
script_summary: Exploring capillary action in plants reveals fascinating science behind their water transport.

segments.voiceover: Ever wondered how plants drink water? It’s not what you think.
Capillary action is a vital process. It allows water to move through tiny channels in plants.
But how does it work? Water molecules cling to each other and to the walls of plant tissues.
This creates a pull that transports water from roots to leaves, even against gravity!
Without capillary action, plants would struggle to survive. Amazing, right?
Curious about more plant mysteries? Hit that follow for your next science fix!
